name: TEBR
summary: TEBR is Haig's solo electronic alias and research persona — violin, voice, and generative AI in collision. Born from the Localization Gap experiments, it routes machine failure and cultural residue into deliberate compositional territory. Not a correction of the algorithm. An argument with it.
notes: Image suggestion: close-up of a violin string intersecting a waveform visualization, black bg. Or a still from a TEBR performance, high contrast.

name: MASHROU' LEILA
summary: 14 years. 40+ countries. 500M+ streams. The first Middle Eastern act on the Rolling Stone cover — and one of the most systematically banned bands in MENA history. Sound as political infrastructure: a counter-public built from music, protest, and architectural thinking across six albums, 35,000-person stadiums, and censorship orders that only expanded the audience.
notes: Use the Baalbeck Roman ruins image — the most iconic spatial image of the band. Roman amphitheater as stage, crowd visible.

name: FICTIVE ENVIRONMENTS
summary: The studio identity and operating system for Haig's current NYC practice. Not a brand — a conceptual frame: the built environment as fiction, and fiction as a design method. Parent node for MEKENA, Lede.nyc, and every speculative infrastructure project currently in production. The name itself is the thesis.
notes: Image: the Walaw Studio mark (و) on black, or an architectural rendering of MEKENA — something that reads as a studio rather than a project.

name: MEKENA NYC
summary: Adaptive reuse of a 1920s building in Queens into an artist residency — the spatial argument made literal. The same systems thinking that drives the software and performance work, operating at the scale of an actual building. A Westbeth Safe Haven partner. Built for the musicians, writers, and builders who don't have a room yet.
notes: Keep the current image if it shows the building exterior clearly. Otherwise: warm interior shot, exposed brick or industrial bones visible.

name: SOMETIMES I WAKE UP ELSEWHERE
summary: ~28,000 words of autofiction. 62 entries. No named sections — sequencing does the structural work. Surrealism arrives through physical detail, not announced conceits. Beau and Griffin are literal anchors. The ending stops in ordinary presence rather than resolving. Literary logic translated into software architecture without losing emotional force: a dream cycle that doubles as a structural diagram of exile and desire.
notes: Image: a handwritten or typed manuscript fragment, partially obscured — or a dark room with a single light source. Avoid the Möbius image — too abstract. This is a literary work.

name: THE LOCALIZATION GAP
summary: Forensic audit of cultural and linguistic bias in AI music systems. 427+ tracks generated and annotated via Python API pipelines, red-teaming Suno, Udio, and MusicLM for Arabic maqam erasure, microtonal collapse, and non-Western harmonic resolution failure. Not a complaint. An evidentiary record. The intellectual spine of Walaw Studio's AI research positioning.
notes: Image: a spectrogram of an Arabic maqam melody, or a Python data visualization of generated tracks annotated by tuning deviation. Black bg, data-forward.

name: THE COST OF BEING QUEER AND ARAB
summary: Published in The New York Times. On the intersection of queerness, Arabness, and the geography of shame — written in the year the band played its last concert. Press node for the public argument that had been building for twelve years: once visibility, censorship, and personal risk became impossible to separate, the essay was the form it took.
notes: Current image works. If replacing: the NYT op-ed page rendered large, or a detail of the article headline on black.

name: THE CARTOGRAPHY OF ABSENCE
summary: Autofiction structured as administrative forms — nine Houses, each a different bureaucratic document (visa application, medical intake, census record) filled with impossible answers about memory, displacement, and longing. The form asks for a birthplace; it has no field for a neighborhood that no longer exists. One of the cleanest textual statements of the practice's method: structural form carrying the emotional and political argument.
notes: Image: a bureaucratic form (visa, census, customs) with handwritten or typed answers that are poetic and impossible — ink bleeding at edges, stark contrast.

name: THE CRANE SONG
summary: ~10,000-word literary fiction. An Armenian-Lebanese exile in Brooklyn encounters a broken AI bird from a virtual reconstruction of Beirut. Character: Sarkis Melkonian. Bureaucratic and clinical language used as literary texture, not parody. Memory, machine learning, and the birds that carry the dead home. The story that holds the Localization Gap thesis and the autofiction practice together at their emotional center.
notes: Image: a crane (bird) silhouette against urban light, or a pixelated/glitched rendering of a Beirut roofscape. Something that holds both the natural and the computational.

name: 427 AI TRACKS ARCHIVE
summary: 401+ AI-generated tracks catalogued in a Notion/Excel taxonomy covering emotional arc, arousal, motifs, themes, and valence — built to map what the models could and couldn't generate. The research body beneath the Localization Gap. Systematic world-building through generative music, treating the archive itself as compositional output.
notes: Image: a screenshot of the Notion database with tracks visible, or a grid of waveform thumbnails. Dense, taxonomic, research-forward.

name: THE MEANING STACK
summary: Seven federated GitHub repositories architected as a single cross-disciplinary system: choreography-of-systems, 3D-Beat-Synth, STORYLINES, music-engines, DERIVE, HAH-WAS, photon+. Authorship language (choreography, score, atlas) replaces engineering language (pipeline, framework, engine). The technical framework that proves the practice is architecture all the way down.
notes: Image: the GitHub network graph for the Meaning Stack repos, or a constellation diagram of the seven repos with connecting lines. Black bg.

name: SYSTEMS CHOREOGRAPHY
summary: Core methodology: treating sound, text, image, code, space, and systems as one interconnected inquiry rather than separate disciplines. The diagonal move — working across fields at angles, finding connective tissue rather than specializing vertically or generalizing horizontally. The invisible architecture behind the entire practice.
notes: Image: a Mark Lombardi-style hand-drawn diagram connecting nodes labeled with the six domains — stark, architectural, on paper or black ground.

name: ROLLING STONE MIDDLE EAST COVER
summary: The first Middle Eastern act on the cover of Rolling Stone — a cultural milestone that formalized Mashrou' Leila's crossover into the broader regional and international media imaginary. The image that made the argument legible to an audience that hadn't been paying attention.
notes: Current image is correct — the Rolling Stone cover itself. Keep it.

name: LEDE.NYC
summary: Autonomous civic intelligence platform for New York City. BigQuery backend, hybrid LLM orchestration, zero human editorial intervention. Neighborhood briefings synthesized from municipal datasets. The missing middle between social panic (Nextdoor, Citizen) and static real estate portals (StreetEasy). Features: Lease Due Diligence Engine, Scaffold Duration Forecasting, Block Mood Forecast, Canine Commute Optimizer.
notes: Image: use one of the Lede.nyc Civic Intelligence webp images already in Drive — the UI screenshot with neighborhood data visible.

name: SOPHIE'S WORLD
summary: Early AI short film and Bartlett dissertation companion. Made during the MArch at UCL — one of the first experiments in the practice where code and image met as co-authors rather than tool and maker. The seed of what eventually became the Codeverse and the Localization Gap methods.
notes: Image: a film still — something cinematic, slightly uncanny. If no still exists, use a frame from the dissertation document.

name: ARCHITECTURE IN LOW RES
summary: Bartlett MArch thesis: how does architecture change when mediated through low-resolution screens? Digital perception, pixelation, and the gap between a building and its representation. The seed crystal for much of the later work — once you see this thesis, the portfolio stops looking eclectic and starts looking precise.
notes: Keep current image if it shows the thesis rendering. It reads well.

name: B018 NIGHTCLUB
summary: Architectural work at Bernard Khoury / DW5 on Beirut's iconic B018 nightclub — a club built underground to reference the civil war-era history of the site, designed to disappear into the landscape. Early professional practice at the intersection of spatial, cultural, and political thinking.
notes: Image: the B018 interior or the iconic retractable roof open at night — widely photographed. Atmospheric, architectural.

name: THE LABYRINTH
summary: Recursive novel and script involving Sayat Nova — the Armenian troubadour poet, patron saint of the exile who sings in a language no one else speaks. A story that knows it is a story, and uses that knowledge as a structural device. Companion work to The Annex.
notes: Image: a fragment of Armenian script or a medieval illumination detail — something that holds the weight of Sayat Nova without illustrating him literally.

name: THE ANNEX
summary: Stage design script exploring memory as spatial problem — the room that holds what the body can't. A companion piece to The Labyrinth: where The Labyrinth is recursive and narrative, The Annex is spatial and performative. Both are architectural arguments made in literary form.
notes: Image: a spare stage plan or elevation drawing, hand-rendered or digitally austere.

name: VESSEL ORCHESTRA
summary: Performance at The Metropolitan Museum of Art using found artifacts and resonant objects as instruments — the museum's collection as an orchestra. Sound extracted from objects that weren't made to make sound. Space, history, and the body in acoustic conversation.
notes: Image: the MET performance — ideally a wide shot showing both the space and the performers in relation to artifacts.

name: SPACE TIME TUNING MACHINE
summary: A cross-disciplinary body of work premiered at SXSW and The Broad Museum, Los Angeles. An instrument played not by making sound from silence but in reverse — tuning noise into melody, melody into silence. Architecture as instrument. The conceptual spine: a wandering troubadour with no memory of songs, finding broken instruments, building a machine to guide the way home. Lineage: Basinski → Caretaker → King Tubby → Eno → Pauline Oliveros.
notes: Keep current image if it shows the installation. Strong spatial photograph.

name: YO-YO MA COLLABORATION
summary: Performance in Byblos, Lebanon — 'Ha'oud (I Will Return)' with Yo-Yo Ma, Mashrou' Leila, and Narcy, from the album Notes for the Future. Cello and voice in an ancient Phoenician city, released as a cultural document during a year of extraordinary Lebanese loss.
notes: Image: performance still from Byblos — ideally Yo-Yo Ma and Haig/band visible together. Warm, historic setting.

name: IBN EL LEIL
summary: The band's most internationally legible full-length — baroque pop, Beirut nightlife, risk, and queer futurity. Ibn El Leil (Son of the Night) distilled the aesthetic into its sharpest form: the night as the only time and place where you can be yourself. Premiered at the Barbican, London.
notes: Current image (album cover) is correct.

name: RAASÜK
summary: Second studio album. The moment the band's visual and sonic language became unmistakably iconic: direct, synthetic, theatrical, and politically alert. Raasük (Shackles) — movement constrained and then released. The record that broke the band regionally and set the visual grammar for the next decade of work.
notes: Current image is correct.

name: EL HAL ROMANCY
summary: The EP that pushed the early Mashrou' Leila world into a richer narrative and visual mythology while the audience was rapidly expanding. El Hal Romancy (The Romantic Solution) — bureaucracy as romantic condition. The album that named an aesthetic and began the practice of using form as content.
notes: Current image is correct.

name: PLOT HOUSE
summary: Residential architectural project in Beirut at Bernard Khoury / DW5. Early professional practice — a building that negotiates between the dense urban fabric of Beirut and the program of the private home. Architecture as negotiation rather than assertion.
notes: Image: architectural photograph of the building exterior, or a plan/section drawing.

name: FRANK
summary: News analysis application for media bias detection — the Localization Gap methodology applied to text corpora rather than audio. FRANK surfaces the structural assumptions embedded in how events are framed, sourced, and narrated. A forensic tool for the information layer.
notes: Image: a UI screenshot showing bias analysis output, or a split-screen of two news articles with differential markup.

name: SUNBURN
summary: Climate data sonification installation. Real-time urban weather data translated into a generative score — the city playing itself. A performance where the instrument is the atmosphere and the score is written by meteorological systems. Spatial, ecological, and architectural thinking applied to sound.
notes: Image: a data visualization of weather patterns with superimposed notation, or a performance still in an outdoor/gallery setting.

name: POST-APOCALYPTIC WALKING TOUR
summary: AR performance overlaying future ruins on the streets of Beirut — shot in the weeks after the August 2020 port explosion. A walk through the city as it already was, narrated by what it might become. Augmented reality not as novelty but as grief instrument.
notes: Image: a still from the AR overlay showing ruins superimposed on an actual Beirut street, or a performer walking with a phone/device.

name: SKYLINE OF INSOMNIA
summary: Visual study of nocturnal urban density — Beirut's skyline as insomniac consciousness. The city that never fully sleeps, never fully wakes. A 5,162-word Calvino-influenced literary fiction piece and visual series that reads the city as a body in a state of partial rest.
notes: Image: a long-exposure night photograph of Beirut's skyline or a dense urban view — high contrast, blue-black tones.

name: BUREAUCRATIC SURREALISM
summary: Aesthetic framework and sustained research practice: bureaucratizing the unbureaucratizable. Form as content. The document as literary artifact. The archive as fiction. Connects The Cartography of Absence, The Crane Song, the Body Maintenance Log, and the Localization Gap methodology — all of which use institutional language as a literary device, not as parody but as texture.
notes: Image: a detail of an administrative form (passport, visa, census) with poetic or impossible answers typed/handwritten in. Stark. Slightly uncanny.

name: TELESCODE
summary: Spatial code visualization and interactive mapping tool — a 3D interface for navigating codebase architecture. Where Codeverse Explorer is the conceptual frame, Telescode is the working implementation. Code made walkable, with acetate-layer depth navigation. Architecture as interface.
notes: Image: a screenshot of the 3D codebase visualization — nodes, connections, depth layers visible. Black bg.

name: MENARA
summary: Generative architectural visualization project — and more: MENARA is also a concept for regional music sovereignty infrastructure, a platform for Arabic musical heritage in the age of AI. The name means lighthouse. It occupies the space between a spatial rendering tool and a cultural institution.
notes: Image: a generative architectural rendering — abstract, volumetric, with Arabic geometric motifs or structural echoes. Warm light on dark ground.

name: ITP CAMP PROTOTYPES
summary: Experimental tech objects built during NYU ITP Camp 2024 — physical computing, speculative interfaces, early hardware prototypes. The residency that grounded the practice in a maker culture, adding tactile and embodied thinking to the predominantly conceptual and digital work.
notes: Image: a photograph of the prototypes on a workbench — wires, circuit boards, physical objects with screens or sensors visible.

name: ARTISTIC FREEDOM INITIATIVE
summary: International advocacy campaigns for human rights and creative liberty — documentation of the censorship of Mashrou' Leila and other artists across MENA, translated into institutional pressure and public record. The legal and advocacy infrastructure built around the practice of making work that gets banned.
notes: Image: a campaign graphic or press release visual — clean, institutional, with a sense of formal documentation.

name: SOUAD
summary: Installation and novella exploring the family archive — photographs, objects, documents assembled into a narrative about a woman (Souad) and the gaps in what gets recorded. The document and the absence of document as equal subjects. An early experiment in the bureaucratic surrealism mode.
notes: Image: an installation shot showing photographs or documents arranged spatially, or a detail of the archive materials.

name: THE BEIRUT SCHOOL
summary: Anthology project and performance cycle — a collective term for a body of work (and an album) that attempts to document and transmit a specific cultural moment in Beirut before it became unavailable. The city as school, the practice as curriculum.
notes: Image: a Beirut street photograph, warm and slightly overexposed — the feeling of a city in the middle of something.

name: MATERIAL FOR A NOVEL
summary: Video essay on tragic endings and memory — found footage and first-person narration examining how endings are constructed and retroactively narrated. The research object and the creative output occupying the same frame. An early experiment in the essayistic mode that runs through all the literary work.
notes: Image: a video still — slightly grainy, documentary quality, with text overlay or a face partially visible.

name: HAH-WAS
summary: Hallucination-detection engine for large language models — the Localization Gap methodology applied to the reliability layer of AI systems. HAH-WAS (سياج, 'fence' or 'sieve') measures not just factual error but cultural erasure: the model's confident wrong answer about a maqam, a place name, a practice. One of the clearest statements in the portfolio that cultural erasure in AI is measurable, not accidental.
notes: Replace current image with a UI screenshot showing hallucination detection output — flagged model responses with confidence scores.

name: PLOT #1063 TOWER
summary: Architectural high-rise project in Beirut at Bernard Khoury / DW5 — a tower navigating the density and vertical ambition of post-war Beirut construction. Early professional practice in large-scale urban architecture.
notes: Image: architectural rendering or photograph of the tower — vertical, Beirut skyline context.

name: 2006 RECONSTRUCTION
summary: Post-conflict urban mapping and planning projects in South Lebanon following the 2006 war — conducted during undergraduate studies at AUB. Architecture as humanitarian response; documentation as political act. The earliest instance of the practice engaging with the built environment as a site of political consequence.
notes: Image: a site map or aerial photograph of South Lebanon reconstruction areas — cartographic, infrastructural.

name: NEW YORK VOICES
summary: Commissioned performance at Joe's Pub, New York — an original work for voice, electronics, and spatial sound exploring the layered identities of New York as a city of transit and arrival. The first major NYC performance work after relocating from Beirut.
notes: Image: a performance still from Joe's Pub — stage lighting, performer visible, intimate venue scale.

name: PEOPLE LIKE US PODCAST
summary: Original score and visual identity for the documentary podcast series. Sound and image working together as the identity layer — the practice applied to longform audio at the intersection of cultural journalism and sound design.
notes: Image: the podcast's cover art or a still from a visual episode — strong visual identity, brand-forward.

name: MUSEUM OF ARCHITECTURE
summary: Award-winning AUB undergraduate architectural design project — a museum of architecture designed as a building that performs its own subject. Won the Fawzi Azar Award for architectural excellence at AUB.
notes: Image: the design presentation — renderings, model photographs, or plans. Architectural quality.

name: ARABIC POP 1925–2025
summary: Interactive mapping of a century of Arabic popular music — tracing stylistic evolution, geographic spread, and cultural inflection points from the early recording era to the present. The historical research that contextualizes the Localization Gap and gives TEBR its genealogy.
notes: Image: a timeline visualization or geographic map of Arabic music's spread — data-forward, archival aesthetic.

name: STAGE ARCHITECTURE
summary: Narrative scenography designed for Mashrou' Leila's global tours — stages as architectural propositions, not just platforms. Each tour's visual environment was designed as an argument about the music: industrial, intimate, monumental, or in ruins. Seven years of building temporary architecture for 35,000-person crowds.
notes: Image: a wide shot of a major stage design — crowd visible, architectural elements foregrounded. The Baalbeck or a festival stage.

name: NOWHERE/ELSEWHERE
summary: 108-entry narrative cycle structured into nine Houses — the predecessor to The Cartography of Absence and Sometimes I Wake Up Elsewhere. The project that established the form: bureaucratic structures as containers for the unbureaucratizable. Transit states, non-arrival, the experience of being between.
notes: Image: a photograph of a transit space — airport corridor, ferry terminal, border crossing — slightly motion-blurred.

name: KARDIA
summary: Mobile biofeedback instrument — React Native, AudioKit (iOS), Oboe (Android), React Native Skia. Heart rate data mapped to generative sound via the Iso-Principle: match the user's physiological state, then guide toward clarity as the body calms. Excavation, not addition — start from dense harmonic chaos, calm the body to reveal the music. The body as compositional input.
notes: Image: a smartphone screen showing the Kardia UI — the three-zone vertical drag model, heart rate abstracted into waveform texture.

name: LOOP CORRIDOR
summary: Meditative art game: bureaucratic purgatory corridors that mutate across loop counts. A virtual architectural space where the environment changes based on repetition — each pass through the corridor slightly different from the last, accumulating distortion. Space, sound, and time as a feedback system.
notes: Image: a first-person perspective screenshot from inside the corridor — slightly uncanny, architectural, with visible mutations or distortions.

name: 3D-BEAT-SYNTH
summary: Spatial audio modulation interface: Three.js + Web Audio API. Sound positioned in 3D space and navigated through — the synthesizer becomes a room you walk around inside. One of the most immediately legible proofs in the portfolio that technical experimentation can stay conceptually clean and experientially immediate.
notes: Keep or replace with a cleaner WebGL screenshot — the 3D interface with sound nodes visible in space.

name: STORYLINES
summary: Graph-based narrative tool — a relational interface for building and navigating story structures where relationships between nodes define meaning rather than linear sequence. Shows how abstract ideas can be translated into a working relational interface that remains legible, surprising, and technically credible.
notes: Keep current image if it shows the graph clearly. Strong visual.

name: ARCHITECTURES OF BELONGING
summary: Research project on spatial agency and identity — how built environments either enable or deny belonging, with particular attention to diasporic and displaced communities. Academic and practice-based research that forms the theoretical underpinning of MEKENA NYC.
notes: Image: a research document spread, or a spatial diagram mapping belonging across a building or neighborhood.

name: THE CODEX PROJECT
summary: Visualizing hidden metadata in archives — a tool for surfacing what documents contain beyond their ostensible content: timestamp patterns, authorship traces, revision histories, embedded coordinates. The archive made legible at a layer below the text.
notes: Image: a visualization of document metadata — connection maps, timestamp clusters, revision trees. Data-forward.

name: SPECULATIVE NARRATIVE DESIGN
summary: Future-casting workshop materials — methodologies for designing with uncertainty, building narratives for futures that don't exist yet. Part of the Fictive Environments practice: treating fiction as a design tool for institutional and spatial problems.
notes: Image: a workshop spread — post-its, diagrams, hands visible around a table. Or a speculative timeline visualization.

name: THE AUTOPSY / BEIRUT PHANTOM
name_change_note: NAME CHANGED: was 'THE AUTOPSY / BEIRUT PHANTOM' — clarifying as two linked works
summary: A spatial media work occupying the territory between architectural rendering and interactive fiction — the 'autopsy' of a city and the 'phantom' of its continuation. Operating on the same low-resolution, fragment-aware logic that runs through the rest of the practice: what remains when the building is gone.
notes: Keep current screenshot if it shows the spatial interface clearly.

name: ARCHIVE OF UNWRITTEN SONGS
summary: Generative art project on melodic loss — an archive populated with songs that were almost written, tunes that dissolved before notation, melodic fragments from the research process. The counterfactual as archive. What the model refused to generate, stored as documentation of the refusal.
notes: Image: a visual representation of absence — empty notation staves, a spectrogram of silence, or a database with deliberately empty fields.

name: VESSEL ORCHESTRA (MET)
summary: Documentation node for the Vessel Orchestra performance at The Metropolitan Museum of Art — photographs, sound recordings, curatorial notes. The museum as resonant space, the collection as instrument, the visitor as listener.
notes: Image: the MET performance — ideally a wide architectural shot of the performance space with performers and objects.

name: CODEVERSE EXPLORER
summary: 3D cartography for visual codebases — spatial computing interface for code architecture using acetate-layer depth navigation. Extends the relational-spatial method of STORYLINES into engineering infrastructure: code as architecture, the repository as a building you can walk through. Makes the practice's cross-domain consistency obvious.
notes: Keep current screenshot — it reads well as spatial code navigation.

name: THE ERASURE PROTOCOLS
summary: Interactive fiction archive documenting protocols of cultural erasure — in AI systems, in censorship regimes, in the archive's own logic of inclusion and exclusion. Draws directly from the Localization Gap research and the experience of systematic banning across MENA.
notes: Image: a redacted document — heavy black bars over text, bureaucratic formatting. Or a database with systematically missing fields.

name: INʿIṬĀF (THE TURN)
summary: Progressive Web App ritual generator — a daily practice tool built on the concept of inʿiṭāf, the Arabic term for a turn or deflection in classical rhetoric. The app generates ritual instructions for reorientation: spatial, temporal, attentional. Systems Choreography as daily practice.
notes: Image: a smartphone screen showing a ritual instruction, or the app's interface — minimal, typographic, Arabic and English text.

name: TUNING FORK
summary: Image decay and visualization software — a tool that applies controlled degradation to images, simulating the effects of time, compression, and transmission error. Architecture in Low Res applied as software: what does an image retain when resolution is deliberately withdrawn?
notes: Image: a before/after comparison of image decay — or a single deeply degraded image that retains enough to be recognizable.

name: HEROES OF A TRANSITIONAL TIME
summary: Visual art critique on masculinity, produced during the Marra.tein Residency in Beirut. An exhibition-based work examining the construction of heroism and masculine identity in post-war Lebanese visual culture.
notes: Image: an installation shot from the exhibition — ideally showing work on walls with the gallery space visible.

name: AUB ARCHITECTURE (B.ARCH)
summary: Five years of architectural education at the American University of Beirut — foundational training in spatial thinking, structural systems, and the specific grammar of Lebanese urbanism. The degree that built the spatial logic underlying all six domains of the practice. Projects included the Museum of Architecture, Bourj Hammoud urban research, and post-conflict reconstruction mapping.
notes: Image: the AUB campus or a studio model from the undergraduate years — architectural, Beirut light.

name: THE BARTLETT (M.ARCH)
summary: Master of Architecture at The Bartlett, UCL London — Said Foundation Scholar, British Lebanese Association Scholar. The thesis: Architecture in Low Res. The degree that connected the spatial practice to digital mediation theory and set the conditions for the code-based work that followed.
notes: Image: the Bartlett building exterior, or a thesis presentation image — London, academic, precise.

name: MARRA.TEIN RESIDENCY
summary: Art residency and exhibition at the Marra.tein space in Hamra, Beirut — an adaptive reuse project itself, an early experiment in the kind of institutional space MEKENA NYC later became. Site of the Heroes of a Transitional Time exhibition.
notes: Image: the Marra.tein space — the building exterior or an exhibition installation view.

name: HOME WORKSPACE (HWP)
summary: Ashkal Alwan's Home Workspace Program — Beirut's most rigorous interdisciplinary residency, focused on critical practice and political art. The program that formalized the practice's engagement with theory, documentation, and the relationship between art and institution.
notes: Image: the Ashkal Alwan space or a documentation photograph from the program — Beirut, institutional, critical.

name: NYU KEVORKIAN RESIDENCY
summary: Artist-in-residence at NYU's Kevorkian Center for Near Eastern Studies — culminating in The Great Gig in the Sky exhibition. The residency that marked the transition of the practice to New York and grounded it in an institutional relationship with Near Eastern Studies.
notes: Image: the Kevorkian Center or the Great Gig exhibition installation — New York, academic, cultural.

name: BANKSY / BLOCK 9 RETREAT
summary: Creative collaboration retreat connected to Banksy's Walled Off Hotel in Palestine — the site of the 'Salam' recording with Roisin Murphy. A convergence of music, visual art, and political space in a site specifically constructed to make the absurdity of the wall literal.
notes: Image: the Walled Off Hotel exterior or interior — Banksy's work visible, the wall in background.

name: AUB 150TH DOCUMENTARY
summary: Visual and music collaboration for the American University of Beirut's 150th anniversary documentary — original score and visual contribution to an institutional history of one of the Arab world's most significant universities.
notes: Image: a documentary film still — AUB campus, archival footage quality.

name: THE GREAT GIG IN THE SKY
summary: Exhibition at the NYU Kevorkian Center — named after the Pink Floyd track, the exhibition examined sonic, spatial, and cultural dimensions of exile and the afterlife of places. The work that established the New York institutional relationship.
notes: Image: the exhibition installation view — works on walls, institutional gallery space, NYU context.

name: VARIATIONS ON CRANES
summary: Video performance piece on the crane as figure — the construction crane ubiquitous in post-war Beirut, and the crane bird that carries the dead home in Armenian mythology. The two cranes as one image, inhabiting the same temporal and spatial register.
notes: Image: a still from the video — either a construction crane against Beirut sky, or the migratory crane bird in flight. Ideally both in the same frame.

name: HOW TO WALK WITH FRIENDS
summary: Video work on public space agency — instructions for moving through the city together, filmed during the 2019–2020 Lebanese uprising. The street as practice space, the walk as political act.
notes: Image: a still from the video — people walking together in a Beirut street, slightly documentary in quality.

name: AUDITIONS FOR A BATTLE
summary: Performance and video art piece — the battle as audition, the audition as performance. An examination of competitive structures and the theater of conflict in post-war Beirut cultural life.
notes: Image: a performance still — theatrical, confrontational, video-art quality.

name: KABOOS (KALEIDOSCOPE)
summary: Early stop-motion visual work — Kaboos means nightmare in Arabic. One of the earliest moving image experiments, made during the undergraduate years at AUB. The kaleidoscope as a structure for the nightmare: repetition, fragmentation, and the failure to escape the pattern.
notes: Image: a still from the stop-motion animation — fragmented, kaleidoscopic, handmade quality.

name: LILLY DOESN'T CARE ANYMORE
summary: Early 2D stop-motion experiment — the first moving image work in the archive. Lilly's indifference as a formal quality: what happens when a character refuses to perform emotional legibility.
notes: Image: a still from the 2D animation — hand-drawn, early, charming in its roughness.

name: MATERIAL FOR A NOVEL (RESEARCH PIECE)
summary: The research companion to the video essay Material for a Novel — notes, source materials, and documentation of the inquiry into tragic endings and narrative construction. The process made visible as artifact.
notes: Image: a research spread — notes, stills, source materials. Documentary quality.

name: POST-CONFLICT URBANISM
summary: Research and urban mapping in South Lebanon following the 2006 war — the earliest instance of the practice engaging with documentation as political act. Architecture as humanitarian response, at the scale of a destroyed landscape.
notes: Image: a map or aerial photograph of South Lebanon — cartographic, infrastructural, documentary.

name: FAWZI AZAR AWARD
summary: The Fawzi Azar Award for Architectural Excellence, received at AUB for the Museum of Architecture project. The first formal recognition in the practice — early evidence that the work was already doing something the field could identify as significant.
notes: Image: the award certificate or a photograph from the ceremony — institutional, formal.

name: TOROS ROSLIN AWARD
summary: Early recognition in the visual arts — the Toros Roslin Award, named after the 13th-century Armenian illuminator, received in 2004 at the beginning of the undergraduate years. An award with deep Armenian cultural significance in the Lebanese Armenian community.
notes: Image: the award documentation or a detail of the illuminated manuscript tradition the award references.

name: FASATEEN (MV)
summary: Breakthrough music video for Mashrou' Leila — Fasateen (Dresses) became the song that introduced the band to a regional audience, with a video that was as much a statement as the song itself.
notes: Image: a still from the Fasateen video — the most recognizable visual moment.

name: RAKSIT LEILA (MV)
summary: Iconic music video and performance — Raksit Leila (Leila's Dance) became one of the band's defining early visual statements. The performance mode, the femininity, the street setting — all of it established.
notes: Image: a still from the video — Leila dancing, the street, the early visual language.

name: DOHA TRIBECA FILM FESTIVAL
summary: Performance and screening at the Doha Tribeca Film Festival — an early international platform for the band, connecting Mashrou' Leila's sound and visual world to the regional film industry.
notes: Image: a performance still or festival documentation photograph.

name: EXIT FESTIVAL
summary: Performance at the Exit Festival in Serbia — one of the earliest European festival appearances for Mashrou' Leila, the beginning of a touring circuit that would eventually cover 40 countries.
notes: Image: a stage shot from Exit Festival — crowd, stage, festival atmosphere.

name: PALÉO FESTIVAL
summary: Performance at the Paléo Festival in Nyon, Switzerland — one of Europe's largest open-air festivals. Mashrou' Leila bringing Arabic-language alternative rock to a Swiss outdoor festival crowd.
notes: Image: a stage shot or crowd photograph from Paléo.

name: NPR TINY DESK
summary: The NPR Tiny Desk Concert — the performance that broke Mashrou' Leila to an English-speaking global audience. Recorded in Washington, D.C., it became one of the most-viewed Tiny Desk concerts featuring a non-English-language act.
notes: Image: the Tiny Desk setup — the band crowded around desks, the informal intimacy of the format.

name: ROMAN (MV)
summary: The anchor image of the band's late visual era — Roman became a feminist and queer manifesto in video form, with the most precise visual language of any Mashrou' Leila music video. Haig's creative direction at its most considered. The door into the broader music video archive.
notes: Keep current image — the Roman video still is exactly right.

name: YSL MUSEUM OPENING
summary: Private performance at the opening of the Yves Saint Laurent Museum in Marrakech — a collaboration between the band's cultural reach and the museum's positioning at the intersection of French colonial aesthetics and Moroccan heritage.
notes: Image: a performance still from Marrakech, or the YSL Museum exterior.

name: MONTREUX JAZZ FESTIVAL
summary: Performance at the Montreux Jazz Festival in Switzerland — one of the world's most prestigious jazz and world music platforms, adding institutional legitimacy to the band's European festival circuit.
notes: Image: a stage shot from Montreux — the lake backdrop, the festival staging.

name: BEIRUT BLAST RELIEF
summary: Global fundraising concert organized in response to the August 4, 2020 Beirut port explosion — Mashrou' Leila's last major collective action as a band, mobilizing international networks for humanitarian response.
notes: Image: a documentary photograph from the post-blast Beirut context, or a concert flyer/poster.

name: GREENPEACE CAMPAIGN
summary: Renewable energy awareness campaign with Greenpeace — Mashrou' Leila lending the band's platform to environmental advocacy, an early instance of the systematic use of cultural reach for institutional change.
notes: Image: the campaign visual — clean, environmental, advocacy-forward.

name: BODY MAINTENANCE LOG
summary: Document art on visa status, health records, and bodily administration — the body as bureaucratic object. A personal archive of the paperwork that mediates existence in a country where you're not from: medical forms, visa renewals, insurance documents treated as literary artifacts. Bureaucratic surrealism applied to the self.
notes: Image: a detail of the actual documents — visa stamp, medical form, insurance card — arranged as a composition.

name: PROJECT ZERO
summary: Civic protocol concept for collective stillness — a framework for institutional and public pause. What does it mean to build a protocol around doing nothing: no notifications, no movement, no production. The civic as contemplative infrastructure.
notes: Image: a blank form with 'Project Zero' as a header, or a visualization of collective stillness — absence as content.

name: THE MIRAGE ROOM
summary: Optical illusion installation produced during the Bartlett/early London years — a room designed to produce spatial disorientation, where surfaces that appear solid are reflective and distances are systematically misread.
notes: Image: an installation photograph — the optical effect visible, the spatial disorientation palpable.

name: LAVA OBJECT
summary: Industrial design piece — an object that references volcanic form and material weight. One of the few industrial design works in the archive, produced during the DW5 period in Beirut.
notes: Image: the object itself, photographed as a product — dark material, sculptural. Removed from atlas per review.

name: PHIL & JOE BARBERSHOP
summary: Interior design for a Beirut barbershop — a commercial interior project at the intersection of craft, cultural space, and everyday architecture. One of the few projects in the archive that is unambiguously domestic and local in scale.
notes: Image: the interior of the barbershop — warm, craft-forward, Beirut neighborhood scale.

name: CERF VOLANT
summary: Lighting installation — Cerf Volant means kite in French. A light object that takes flight, literally or in spatial metaphor. An early experiment in atmospheric and environmental design.
notes: Image: the installation lit — light quality, space transformed. Removed from atlas per review.

name: BOURJ HAMMOUD THESIS
summary: AUB undergraduate thesis on Bourj Hammoud — the Armenian-Lebanese quarter of Beirut, one of the most densely layered urban environments in the Arab world. Urban research at the intersection of heritage, density, and diaspora identity.
notes: Image: a map of Bourj Hammoud, or a photograph of the neighborhood — dense, layered, Armenian street signage visible.

name: MAP OF THE WOUND
summary: Composite literary and visual work — a map drawn in the shape of a wound, or a wound described with the precision of a map. Documentation of a specific kind of damage that has both geographic and somatic coordinates.
notes: Image: a detail of the map — hand-drawn, anatomical precision applied to geographic form.

name: ANTS DESIGN STORE
summary: Commercial interior for a design store in Beirut and Yerevan — a retail environment for design objects, spanning Lebanese and Armenian contexts. One of the few projects that literally bridges the two cities that anchor Haig's identity.
notes: Image: the store interior — objects on display, clean retail design, Beirut or Yerevan context.

name: SKOUN CONCERT
summary: Mental health awareness concert — Mashrou' Leila in partnership with Skoun, Lebanon's first drug treatment and harm reduction center. The band's platform used for a conversation about mental health in a cultural context where that conversation was rare.
notes: Image: a concert still or campaign visual — warm, advocacy-forward.

name: MARSA CLINIC EVENT
summary: Sexual health awareness event with Marsa, Lebanon's only sexual health clinic for at-risk populations. The band's platform in service of public health advocacy in a country without formal LGBTQ+ recognition.
notes: Image: an event visual or poster — clinical clarity, warm accessibility.

name: SAWA AID CONCERT
summary: Syrian refugee fundraising concert — Mashrou' Leila mobilizing cultural platform for humanitarian response to the Syrian displacement crisis at its peak.
notes: Image: a concert still or campaign poster — humanitarian urgency, cultural warmth.

name: SEAL CONCERT
summary: SEAL (Secular Arab Artists for Lebanon) concert — a coalition performance for economic reform and anti-corruption advocacy in Lebanon. The band as political actor in the most direct sense: using concert infrastructure for civic pressure.
notes: Image: a concert still or SEAL coalition visual.

name: TORONTO PRIDE
summary: Performance at Toronto Pride — one of the world's largest Pride celebrations, marking the band's explicit alignment with LGBTQ+ advocacy on the global stage and in the diaspora.
notes: Image: a Pride performance still — rainbow flags, crowd, stage.

name: OSLO WORLD
summary: Performance at the Oslo World music festival in Norway — a curated international music festival specifically focused on global and non-Western music, an institutional validation of the band's positioning.
notes: Image: a stage shot from Oslo World — intimate festival scale.

name: LOWLANDS FESTIVAL
summary: Performance at the Lowlands Festival in the Netherlands — one of Europe's major multi-day festivals, the band playing to an audience with no existing knowledge of Arabic-language rock.
notes: Image: a stage shot from Lowlands — festival scale, crowd visible.

name: HERCULES & LOVE AFFAIR
summary: Collaboration: 'Are You Still Certain?' — Mashrou' Leila featured on the Hercules & Love Affair album Omnion. A convergence of the band's Arabic-language queer politics and the New York house music tradition.
notes: Image: the Omnion album cover, or a performance still of the collaboration.

name: ROISIN MURPHY
summary: Collaboration: 'Salam' — recorded at Banksy's Walled Off Hotel in Palestine. Roisin Murphy and Mashrou' Leila in a politically charged site, making a song about peace in the most ironic location possible.
notes: Image: a still from the Salam recording session at the Walled Off Hotel.

name: LOST SOULS OF SATURN
summary: Collaboration: 'Bint el Khandaq' — Mashrou' Leila featured on the Lost Souls of Saturn project by Seth Troxler and Phil Moffa. The band's voice in an electronic music context, stripping the arrangement back to the essential.
notes: Image: the Lost Souls of Saturn release artwork, or a DJ performance still.

name: MIKA COLLABORATION
summary: Collaboration: 'Promiseland' — performed at the I Love Beirut benefit concert in the ruins of the Sursock Palace after the August 2020 explosion. Mika and Mashrou' Leila in the rubble of one of Beirut's most iconic heritage buildings, singing about a promised land.
notes: Image: the Sursock Palace performance still — the ruined palace, the performers, the emotional weight of the site.

name: THE NARCYCIST
summary: Collaboration: 'Time' — with The Narcycist (Yassin Alsalman), the Iraqi-Canadian rapper and poet. A convergence of Arabic diaspora musical traditions across different geographic and stylistic registers.
notes: Image: a studio or performance still from the collaboration.

name: CAVALRY (MV)
summary: Music video on militarized space — Cavalry as a visual argument about the aestheticization of military presence in public life, with the band occupying and reclaiming militarized visual grammar.
notes: Image: the most striking still from the Cavalry video — the militarized aesthetic, reclaimed.

name: RADIO ROMANCE (MV)
summary: Narrative music video — Radio Romance as a story told in the specific emotional register of late-night radio and the kind of love that only exists in that frequency.
notes: Image: a still from the Radio Romance video — warm, nocturnal, narrative quality.

name: AOEDE (MV)
summary: Experimental visual video — Aoede, the muse of song, rendered in an experimental visual language that departs from the band's usual aesthetic. A more abstract, image-driven approach.
notes: Image: a still from the Aoede video — abstract, experimental, the most visually unexpected frame.

name: 3 MINUTES (MV)
summary: Fan-collaborated video — 3 Minutes was built from footage submitted by fans across MENA, making the audience the image-makers. The most participatory and distributed visual work in the Mashrou' archive.
notes: Image: a still from the fan video — assembled, participatory, multiple sources visible in the aesthetic.

name: LIL WATAN (MV)
summary: Political satire music video — Lil Watan (For the Homeland) is the band's sharpest political satire, a video that treats Lebanese political culture with the precision of a surgical instrument and the timing of a joke.
notes: Image: the most satirically charged still from the Lil Watan video.

name: THE WAITING ROOM
summary: Early architectural installation — the waiting room as spatial condition, as political state, as the universal experience of institutional suspension. Bureaucratic surrealism as spatial practice, before it had a name.
notes: Image: the installation — a waiting room rendered uncanny, chairs and light and the specific quality of bureaucratic time.

name: 1000 STRINGS AT REST
summary: Multimedia performance and installation — 1000 strings, each at rest, each holding the potential of a sound that has not yet been made. A work that bridges Mashrou' Leila, the Space Time Tuning Machine, and DERIVE: the archive of the not-yet-played. Subtractive composition at its most literal.
notes: Replace current image (Ibn El Leil album cover) with an installation photograph of the strings-at-rest work.

name: GALAXY BIRDS
summary: Digital and generative art piece — birds rendered in generative code, forming constellations and dispersing. The bird as unit of both natural system and computational system, migrating through algorithmic space.
notes: Image: a frame from the generative piece — birds as particles or nodes, constellation-forming, black bg.

name: PHOTON+
summary: Optical sequencer — light as both signal and score. Photon+ translates architectural logic (rhythm, interval, proportion) directly into an electronic instrument: the building plan becomes the step sequencer. One of the most elegant examples of architectural thinking translated into an instrument.
notes: Keep current image if it shows the interface clearly. Strong visual.

name: DERIVE
summary: Memory as emotional adjacency rather than chronology — DERIVE (from the Situationist dérive) turns fragmented archives into a navigable, performable system. You don't search the archive by date or keyword; you drift through it by association, following the emotional logic of connection rather than the administrative logic of filing.
notes: Keep current image — strong visual of the navigation system.

name: MÖBIUS ENGINE
summary: Logic and code prototype — a computational system that has no inside or outside, where input and output occupy the same surface. A thought experiment about feedback, recursion, and the systems that contain themselves.
notes: Image: a visualization of the Möbius topology in code — a rendered surface, or a diagram of the recursive logic.

name: RESONANCE ATLAS
summary: Spatial sound map — the atlas that names the broader field that several projects are already operating within. Sound positioned in geographic and emotional space, the connections between tracks made navigable. The research archive as a terrain you can walk through.
notes: Replace current image (portfolio-tracks) with a spatial map visualization — geographic or emotional terrain with sound nodes marked.

name: GHOST CITY
summary: Data-driven visual piece — Beirut rendered in negative space, the city as an absence defined by the data of what was there. A ghost city: the data that survives after the physical city is damaged, destroyed, or inaccessible.
notes: Image: the data visualization — Beirut in negative space, the ghost of the city visible in the data patterns.

name: BEAU & GRIFFIN
summary: Personal documentation — Beau (cat) and Griffin (dachshund), recurring presences in the creative work and daily life. The non-human companions who appear in the autofiction, the apartment, and the background of every working photograph. Not a project exactly, but a node in the constellation that anchors everything else in the ordinary.
notes: Image: a photograph of Beau and Griffin together — warm, intimate, domestic.

name: NEBUCAT
summary: Conceptual avatar and character — a cat rendered as nebula, or a nebula that is recognizably a cat. An early experiment in character design at the intersection of the personal (Beau) and the astronomical (the constellation system of the portfolio itself).
notes: Image: the Nebucat illustration or render — nebular, feline, conceptual.

name: NON-LINEAR TIME
summary: Visual and logic experiment — time rendered as a spatial problem rather than a sequential one. Moments positioned by emotional proximity rather than chronological order. The technical argument for how the DERIVE system and the autofiction manuscripts handle time.
notes: Image: a visualization of non-linear time — moments as nodes in a spatial arrangement, connections drawn by proximity rather than sequence.

name: THE ARCHIVE
summary: Meta-project on documentation — the archive that documents the process of building an archive. A recursive structure: the project that contains notes on how all the other projects were assembled, preserved, and made navigable.
notes: Image: a screenshot of the archive system itself — a database or document structure, the meta-level visible.

name: ARCHITECTING THE LIMINAL
summary: Spatial research paper on liminal spaces — the threshold, the corridor, the space between states. The theoretical text that sits behind MEKENA NYC, the Loop Corridor, and the entire practice of designing for transition rather than arrival.
notes: Image: the paper itself, or a diagram of liminal space — the threshold as spatial condition.

name: 401 SONGS DATABASE
summary: Technical music archive — 401 AI-assisted tracks organized in a Notion/Excel taxonomy covering emotional arc, arousal, motifs, themes, and valence. The technical complement to the 427 AI Tracks Archive: where that is the research body, this is the production database.
notes: Image: a screenshot of the database — the taxonomy structure visible, tracks organized by emotional arc.

name: LOUVRE ABU DHABI
summary: Internship and architectural research at Ateliers Jean Nouvel, contributing to the Louvre Abu Dhabi project — one of the most significant architectural commissions of the 21st century. The building that applied Islamic geometric principles to a contemporary museum program.
notes: Image: the Louvre Abu Dhabi under construction or completed — Jean Nouvel's latticed dome, light filtering through.

name: COLORS BABY
summary: AI-generated music video — a systematic study in AI video generation aesthetics, temporal coherence, and the gap between prompt and image. Produced using Suno, Sora, Kling, Veo, and Minimax. The production experiment that preceded the 1001 Nights Film.
notes: Image: a striking still from the Colors Baby video — generative aesthetic, color-forward, AI-visible in the texture.

name: MAQAMAI
summary: Maqam tuning and reference tool — filling the human infrastructure gap between recognizing model failure (the Localization Gap) and being able to hear what's missing. MAQAMAI makes Arabic maqam systems audible, navigable, and comparable: a reference instrument for the research.
notes: Image: the MAQAMAI interface — maqam scales visualized, tuning reference visible. Clean, musical, technical.

name: HOUSE II: SKIN
summary: Architectural installation and model exploring the skin of a building as its primary medium — the building as body, with a surface that registers touch, weather, and time. An early experiment in the somatic dimension of architectural design.
notes: Image: the architectural model — the skin surface, material quality, the somatic dimension visible.

name: THE FORGERY
summary: Narrative and code concept — a forgery as the most sincere form of document, a copy that reveals more about the original than the original reveals about itself. Interactive fiction about authenticity, attribution, and the institutional life of objects.
notes: Image: a detail of a forged document — the seam where the forgery becomes visible, the almost-convincing authentication mark.

name: PROJECTARIUM AI
summary: AI tool for project management — an A&R agent and project tracker built on the premise that creative project management requires different logic than production management. Not deadlines and deliverables but emotional arcs and conceptual dependencies.
notes: Image: a screenshot of the Projectarium interface — project cards with emotional arc and conceptual dependency fields visible.

name: BINT JBEIL REHABILITATION
summary: Heritage reconstruction project in Bint Jbeil, South Lebanon — one of the towns most heavily damaged in the 2006 war. Architectural research and planning for the rehabilitation of heritage structures, conducted during undergraduate studies at AUB.
notes: Image: a photograph of Bint Jbeil's heritage architecture, pre or post reconstruction — the town, the traditional building fabric.

name: BEIRUT RIVER PROJECT
summary: Urban revitalization design for the Beirut River — the neglected waterway that runs through the city as a concrete channel, historically and ecologically severed from the urban life around it. An AUB thesis project proposing a reintegration of the river into the city's spatial and ecological fabric.
notes: Image: a site map or rendering of the Beirut River project — the river visible, the urban fabric around it.

name: OPERA HOUSE DESIGN
summary: Large-scale architectural design for an opera house — an AUB undergraduate project grappling with the program of cultural infrastructure in a city without one. The first experiment in designing for a public that doesn't yet have the space to become a public.
notes: Image: the architectural design — a rendering or model of the opera house, the spatial ambition of the program visible.

name: BARBICAN RELEASE
summary: Major performance event in London at the Barbican Centre — the release concert for Ibn El Leil, Mashrou' Leila's most internationally legible album, in one of the world's most significant arts institutions. The band at its most European-facing.
notes: Image: a performance still from the Barbican — the hall, the stage, the audience, the scale.

name: CANNES PRIVATE PARTY
summary: Private performance at the Cannes Film Festival — Mashrou' Leila at the intersection of Arabic cultural production and the global film industry, performing for an audience already predisposed to the idea of Arab artistic expression.
notes: Image: a Cannes performance still or the festival context — the Palais, the Mediterranean light, the band.

name: FESTIVAL TRANSART
summary: Performance at Festival Transart in Bolzano, Italy — a festival at the intersection of music, performance, and visual art, in a South Tyrolean city at the crossroads of Italian and German culture. Mashrou' Leila in a context defined by its own questions about identity and belonging.
notes: Image: a performance still from Bolzano — the Alpine context, the festival scale.

name: CAIRO MUSIC PARK
summary: Performance in Egypt at the Cairo Music Park — one of the band's rare performances in the Arab world's most populous country, at a moment when Egypt was both a major audience and a source of significant political risk.
notes: Image: a performance still from Cairo — the Egyptian crowd, the scale, the political weight of the geography.

name: SHIM EL YASMEEN
summary: Foundation song and the beginning — Shim El Yasmeen (Smell the Jasmine) was the first song that defined what Mashrou' Leila could be: Arabic-language, queer-inflected, melodically immediate. The song before the band was a band.
notes: Image: an early performance still or the self-titled EP cover — the rawness of the beginning.

name: ABSOLUT TRANSFORM
summary: Brand and music collaboration with Absolut Vodka's Transform Today campaign — Mashrou' Leila as one of the global artists selected for the initiative. An early instance of the band's cultural reach being leveraged by international brands.
notes: Image: the campaign visual — Absolut aesthetic, the band's image in the campaign context.

name: WALAW MANIFESTO
summary: The origin text of Walaw Studio — the manifesto written in 2021 that named the practice and defined its terms. The و (waw) of Walaw: the Arabic letter that reads simultaneously as waveform, heartbeat spike, and hook. The document that made the studio possible by making it legible.
notes: Image: the Walaw Studio logo (و) large on black, or the first page of the manifesto — the name and the signal together.

name: THE BEIRUT SCHOOL (ALBUM)
summary: The final anthology album — The Beirut School as a cultural document of a specific moment in Beirut's artistic life, assembled as the city was already beginning its long descent into crisis. The album that named a practice and closed a chapter.
notes: Image: the album cover — the visual identity of the Beirut School as a concept.
